results.claim: Campaign completed with 1.262x cumulative E2E speedup across 2 rounds
Summary confirms 26.2% improvement at BS=8
Round 1 shipped OP-002 with 1.0346x E2E speedup, selected from 2 candidates
OP-002 delivered 3.46% E2E improvement at BS=8, best at BS=4 (4.03%), no regressions
Round 2 shipped OP-R2-001 with 1.227x E2E speedup over patched R2 baseline
OP-R2-001 delivered 18.5% improvement at BS=8, consistent 14-18% across all batch sizes
Combined speedup table shows consistent improvement across all batch sizes with peak at BS=8 (1.262x)
Integration analysis confirmed orthogonality between R1 and R2 optimizations
GEMM identified as rank 1 bottleneck: 89.0% of decode at BS=8, 68.8% BW utilization, 1.38x Amdahl ceiling at BF16 peak BW
Weight compression ceilings computed: FP8 at peak BW yields 2.91x GEMM speedup, 2.41x Amdahl E2E at BS=8
Post-OP-002 GEMM share unchanged at 89.2%, FP8 Amdahl ceiling recalculated at 2.34x E2E for BS=8
Summary of what changed after OP-002: GEMM is still 89.2% at BS=8, FP8 at peak BW gives 2.34x E2E
Linear attention layer kernel chain mapped with per-kernel timings: ~423 us total per layer at BS=8 (GEMM 359us, linear attn 42us, overhead 22us)
Source code to kernel mapping links each projection in Qwen3_5DecoderLayer.forward() to specific CUDA kernels with measured timings
Round 1 debate scored 3 champions: Champion-1 at 8.15, Champion-2 at 6.65, Champion-3 at 7.00
Six key debate findings including BV=128 invalidation, in_proj_ba as highest ROI, and Triton vs CUTLASS conclusion
Round 2 selected merged FP8 W8A16 kernel combining best elements from Champions 1 and 2, expected 1.35-1.50x E2E
Key Round 2 findings: FP8 W8A16 is dominant optimization, N-major layout universally better, benchmark methodology matters
Round 1 debate concluded Triton cannot beat CUTLASS, eliminating GEMM as optimization target based on single SwiGLU fusion test
Champion-1 dismissed GEMM using L2-warmed benchmarks showing physically impossible BW utilization (115%, 156%, 171%)
All 3 parallel tracks (OP-001, OP-002, OP-R2-001) passed validation with correctness=true
OP-002 delivered 3.46% E2E improvement at BS=8 with no regressions on any batch size
OP-001 passed all gates but delivered lower 1.17% E2E at BS=8 with 1.30x kernel speedup
OP-002 shipped fused Triton kernel combining in_proj_ba inline, 3x BF16 copy elimination, and gating fusion
Kernel-level verification: net savings of 971.6ms at BS=8, WMMA 128x1 decode instances eliminated, 72 copies/step removed
Kernel performance: Phase 2 at BS=8 saves 34.96 us per call, 4.67x average speedup, 839 us/decode step across 24 layers
FP8 W8A16 delivered 1.81x weighted kernel speedup, 1.227x E2E over patched R2, 1.262x combined over original baseline
Per-shape kernel speedups: gate_up_proj 1.97x, down_proj 1.85x, in_proj_qkvz 1.88x, out_proj 1.32x, qkv_proj 1.73x
Delta math verification: f_GEMM=0.89, kernel_speedup=1.81x, expected 1.66x E2E but actual 1.227x (34.4% efficiency) due to prefill, graph overhead, cache, compile boundaries
OP-001 correctness: Q/K/V bit-exact (zero error), gating error <4e-6, all batch sizes pass
OP-002 correctness: Q/K/V and conv state bit-exact, g/beta errors well within 0.01 relative error kill criterion
OP-R2-001 CUDA graph correctness: all 15 tests passed with exact match (max_diff=0.000000)
Champion-3 claimed 1,107 GB/s GDR BW at BS=8 using wrong dtype (FP32 instead of BF16 state), physically impossible on L40S
Champion-1 self-withdrew BV=128 claim: methodology copied fresh data after L2 flush, corrected experiment shows BV=128 is 3% SLOWER
Bottleneck analysis Section 10 explicitly identified GEMM + weight compression as primary target, yet Round 1 champions ignored it
Round 1 debate generalized from single negative result to exclude all GEMM optimization
Second aborted baseline sweep produced only BS=1 JSON before stopping mid-sweep
OP-001 BS=32 first-iteration outlier (10.91s) required second 10-iteration benchmark run to confirm p50 improvement
OP-002 gated behind VLLM_FUSED_GDN_PREPROCESS=1, default disabled, no production risk
OP-R2-001 enable flags: VLLM_FP8_DECODE_GEMM=1 for FP8 kernel, VLLM_FUSED_GDN_PREPROCESS=1 for OP-002
CUDA graph safety: no allocations within fused kernel, shapes stable per bucket, verified capture+replay identical
OP-R2-001 CUDA graph compatibility: 51 piecewise + 35 full graphs captured successfully, no graph breaks
Hardware: L40S with 733 GB/s peak BW, 362 TFLOPS BF16, CC 8.9
Software: PyTorch 2.10.0+cu128, CUDA 12.8
Round 2 debate selection rationale: FP8 W8A16 merged from champions 1+2 with 1.35-1.50x E2E estimated
Key insight that FP8 W8A16 halves weight bytes to directly address BW bottleneck with measured 1.55-2.13x kernel speedups
After OP-002, analysis confirmed GEMM still dominates at 89.2% with FP8 giving 2.34x Amdahl E2E ceiling, correctly directing Round 2
FP8 decode GEMM confirmed executing via log message: quantized 226 linear layers for decode acceleration
Architecture: 32 layers total with 24 linear_attention (gated delta rule) + 8 full_attention, hidden=2560, intermediate=9216
Weight matrix breakdown: MLP projections 4530MB (largest), linear attn projections 2021MB, full attn 420MB -- total 6970MB in BF16
